title: Publish local main (5ac628b) to origin
description:
Local main is 1 commit ahead of origin with 5ac628b 'chore: initialize overstory and ecosystem tools' (.canopy/, .mulch/, .overstory/, .seeds/, CLAUDE.md, .gitattributes — 27 new files, 2604 lines). Publish to origin so the feature branch can be rebased onto a published target.

ACCEPTANCE
- origin/main points at 5ac628b
- git status reports up to date with origin

NOTES
- Coordinator cannot perform the publish op (write blocked). Human or builder must run the publish.
- Required before the rebase task starts.

title: Rebase claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV onto main, run quality gate, publish
description:
After VIGOR-73da: rebase the unmerged feature branch onto local main, verify the full quality gate, and publish the rebased branch.

CONTEXT
- Feature branch: origin/claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV (commit 0f6a008, +3111 lines, 54 files)
- Ships vigor-agent (configurable agent: AgentConfig schema, FactoryRef registry, Router, AgentOrchestrator, CLI) and vigor-mcp (MCP-as-ToolBackend bridge over stdio/sse/http) plus Open Plugin Spec v1 manifests for existing adapters.
- ADR-0014 covers the AgentConfig design; ADR-0015 covers Open Plugin Spec compatibility.
- Diff against local main shows ZERO file overlap with 5ac628b — rebase expected to apply with no conflicts.
- Runtime change is surgical: RunContext gains optional 'tools: ToolBackend | None = None' and Orchestrator.__init__ gains a matching kwarg. Backward-compatible.

WORK
1. Check out a fresh local branch from origin/claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV.
2. git rebase main (expect clean apply).
3. uv sync --all-packages --all-extras
4. Quality gate (all must pass):
   - uv run ruff check .
   - uv run ruff format --check .
   - uv run mypy
   - uv run pytest
5. If green, publish the rebased branch to origin under the same name.
6. Send merge_ready mail to coordinator with the branch name and quality-gate results.

ACCEPTANCE
- Rebase clean (no manual conflict resolution)
- All four quality-gate steps pass
- Branch published to origin
- merge_ready mail received by coordinator

DO NOT
- Modify the loop semantics in vigor-runtime/orchestrator.py beyond the additive 'tools' threading already on the branch
- Squash the branch's commit history without explicit instruction
- Force-publish over an updated remote without coordinator approval
closeReason: Rebase delivered. The rebased framework branch (commit 04d57d0) was incorporated into VIGOR-b198 (commit 8cb3e74 merged the rebase branch into the fix worktree base, then ea7e0f3 added fixes on top). Final shape on main: 5b2f891 brings framework + all P0/P1 fixes as a single coherent unit.

title: Coordinator-merge rebased agent-adapter-framework branch into main
description:
After VIGOR-67ca: merge the rebased and verified feature branch into main.

ACCEPTANCE
- ov merge --branch <branch-from-merge_ready> --dry-run produces clean output
- ov merge --branch <branch-from-merge_ready> succeeds
- main on origin contains the vigor-agent + vigor-mcp packages and the Open Plugin Spec adapter manifests
- VIGOR-73da and VIGOR-67ca closed referencing this merge

NOTES
- Coordinator-owned task. Do NOT merge before lead/builder sends typed merge_ready mail.
- Watchdog completion nudges or 'ov status' showing builder completed are NOT merge authorization.
closeReason: Framework branch + P0/P1 fixes merged to main as 5b2f891 (preceded by 3cf1f6b harness fix). On main: vigor-agent and vigor-mcp packages, ADR-0014/0015/0016/0017, Open Plugin Spec manifests + generated SKILL.md for all adapters, additive RunContext.tools field, all 4 P0 security/correctness fixes, R1+R2+R3 P1 fixes. 142 tests passing (up from 91 baseline). Two pre-existing optional-SDK fragile tests remain documented out-of-scope.

title: Research: 2026 MCP server landscape for VIGOR reviewer use cases
description:
Phase 3 deep-research item. After the agent-adapter-framework branch lands, vigor-mcp will be the vehicle for attaching external reviewers. Survey the 2026 MCP ecosystem for servers that map onto VIGOR's existing external blockers.

WORK
- VLM aesthetic critic candidates (Claude vision, Anthropic image analyzer, OSS alternatives) — which are MCP-native or trivially wrappable?
- VideoScore2 hard scorer paths — find any 2026 MCP servers exposing video quality scoring; otherwise document the wrap-VideoScore2-as-MCP path.
- CAD mesh/FEM validators — survey openscad/cadquery/freecad MCP servers; identify which exposes mesh validation suitable as a vigor-adapter-cad reviewer.
- AIECF integration — is there a 2026 MCP server for AIECF? If not, document the wrapping cost.
- Security posture per server: tool_allowlist viability, auth model, rate-limit characteristics.

DELIVERABLE
- docs/research/2026-mcp-reviewer-survey.md with: per-server table (name, transport, auth, allowlist coverage, fitness for VIGOR reviewer use), top-3 picks per modality, and a recommended sequencing.

ACCEPTANCE
- File committed to the branch
- At least 2 viable MCP servers identified per modality (photo, video, CAD) OR documented why none exist
- Security posture explicit per recommendation
closeReason: Research stream complete and merged. Output: docs/research/2026-mcp-reviewer-survey.md on main.

title: Research: how Open Plugin Spec v1 plugins are actually consumed by hosts in 2026
description:
Phase 3 deep-research item. ADR-0015 declares VIGOR adapters as Open-Plugin-Spec-compliant plugins. We need empirical evidence that they actually load in the hosts that matter.

WORK
- Test-load each adapter's .plugin/plugin.json + skills/ in: Claude Code, Hermes, Goose, Strands, Gemini CLI, Sourcegraph Amp, JetBrains Junie, Block Goose. Document which work, which fail, which need a vendor-specific manifest path.
- For each host: where does the host expect MCP servers to be declared? Does it use .plugin/.mcp.json, or its own format?
- Document which hosts need an additional .claude-plugin/plugin.json or similar vendor path (ADR-0015 'Defer' decision — re-evaluate based on actual host behavior).
- Find any breaking changes in OPS v1 since 2026-05-01 (the ADR date).

DELIVERABLE
- docs/research/open-plugin-spec-host-compatibility.md with: per-host loading matrix, MCP declaration paths per host, recommended additional manifest paths VIGOR should ship.

ACCEPTANCE
- All 8 hosts attempted (note unavailable hosts explicitly)
- At least 4 hosts confirmed loading
- Concrete recommendation on whether to ship additional vendor manifest paths
closeReason: Research stream complete and merged. Output: docs/research/open-plugin-spec-host-compatibility.md on main. Major finding (zero hosts honor vendor-neutral path) feeds ADR-0017.

title: Architect: ADR-0016 official MCP servers + security posture
description:
Phase 4 ADR. Once the MCP reviewer survey lands, write the ADR that makes the picks official.

CONTENT
- Decide which MCP servers VIGOR officially supports as reviewers (per modality).
- Define the security posture: required tool_allowlist scopes, default timeouts, recommended transport per use case (stdio for local validators, http/sse for hosted critics).
- Define what 'official support' means — version range, CI smoke test, response schema validation.
- Reject alternatives explicitly (custom-built adapters per provider, bypassing MCP entirely).
- Cite evidence from the MCP reviewer survey research doc.

DEPENDENCIES
- Blocked by the MCP reviewer survey research

ACCEPTANCE
- docs/adr/0016-official-mcp-servers.md committed in MADR 3.0 format
- ≥2 considered options per decision point
- Security posture concrete (not aspirational)
- Status: proposed (promote to accepted with user signoff)
closeReason: ADR-0016 merged to main. Status: proposed (promote to accepted with user signoff). 8 decision points (Alt-A..Alt-H), security posture concrete (per-server tool_allowlists, default timeouts, transport policy), citations from 2026-mcp-reviewer-survey.md and TIGER-Lab/VideoScore2.

title: Architect: ADR-0017 pure-MCP plugin support (deferred from ADR-0014)
description:
Phase 4 ADR. plugin_discovery.load_plugin_directory in v1 only loads plugins that supply a Python FactoryRef via .plugin/vigor.json. Pure-MCP plugins (skills + MCP servers, no Python) are intentionally deferred. Decide the v2 path.

CONTENT
- Option A: Lift the restriction. Allow pure-MCP plugins to register MCP servers but warn that they cannot drive the loop.
- Option B: Add a minimal generic MCP-driven adapter that consumes any pure-MCP plugin's tools as compile/render/review hooks.
- Option C: Defer indefinitely — pure-MCP plugins remain ambient tool sources only via AgentConfig.mcp_servers, not via adapter discovery.
- Trade-offs: discoverability vs. typed-runtime guarantees vs. user-experience.

DEPENDENCIES
- Should be written after the host compatibility research lands so we know how often pure-MCP plugins appear in the wild.

ACCEPTANCE
- docs/adr/0017-pure-mcp-plugin-support.md in MADR 3.0 format
- ≥3 considered options
- Status: proposed
closeReason: ADR-0017 merged to main as 'Admit Pure-MCP Plugins As Ambient Tool Sources'. Status: proposed. Recommends Option A (lift the restriction, pure-MCP plugins become ambient tool sources only). 3 considered options. Promotion to accepted requires VIGOR-8bdf resolution (host-side allowlist for plugin-supplied prefixes) and a host-side MCP-server allowlist. Note: vendor manifest dual-publishing decision (the 0/8 hosts finding from host research) is a SEPARATE topic deserving its own ADR-0018 — filing now.

title: Build: example AgentConfig + end-to-end demo using vigor-agent + vigor-mcp
description:
Phase 6 execution. Once the framework branch is merged, ship a runnable example demonstrating the new configurable agent path.

WORK
- Write examples/agent-demo/ with: agent.yaml declaring all three adapters (photo, video-manim, cad), an echo backend, and at least one MCP server (filesystem MCP is a safe v1 choice).
- Add a runnable script: 'uv run vigor-agent run --config examples/agent-demo/agent.yaml task.json' that succeeds for at least 3 task types (one per modality) routed via modality_match.
- Add an integration test at packages/vigor-agent/tests/test_e2e_demo.py that boots the example AgentConfig with a mock MCP server (use the SessionOpener injection point) and runs all 3 task types end-to-end.
- Update examples/README.md or root README to document the new path.

ACCEPTANCE
- 'uv run vigor-agent run ...' succeeds for 3 tasks
- Integration test passes in CI
- Documented in README

title: Build: scripts/regen_skills CI workflow + drift guard
description:
Phase 6 execution. ADR-0015 cites scripts/regen_skills.py as a CI diff guard against IR-schema/SKILL.md drift. Verify the workflow exists in .github/workflows/ on the merged branch; if missing, add it.

WORK
- Confirm whether .github/workflows/ has a workflow that runs scripts/regen_skills.py and fails on a non-empty git diff.
- If missing: add .github/workflows/skill-drift.yml — runs on PR, executes 'uv run python scripts/regen_skills.py', then 'git diff --exit-code packages/*/skills/'.
- Document in CONTRIBUTING.md: how contributors regenerate skills locally before opening a PR.

ACCEPTANCE
- Workflow exists and triggers on PR
- A test-mutation of any adapter's IR schema causes the workflow to fail
- CONTRIBUTING.md documents the regenerate-then-commit step

title: Build: vigor-mcp integration test against a real local MCP server
description:
Phase 6 execution. The branch ships unit tests with a fake SessionOpener but no integration test against a real MCP process. Add one.

WORK
- Use a lightweight reference MCP server (e.g., the modelcontextprotocol/servers filesystem server, runnable as a subprocess) gated on availability — skip CI if not installed.
- Test the full path: MCPToolBackend.list_tools, call_tool with allowlist, timeout enforcement, aclose cleanup.
- Add a marker (pytest.mark.requires_mcp) and document how to run.

ACCEPTANCE
- Integration test exists at packages/vigor-mcp/tests/test_integration_real.py
- Test passes locally with the chosen reference server installed
- Test is skipped (not failed) when the server is unavailable

title: Review: comprehensive adversarial review of agent-adapter-framework branch
description:
Phase 7 concurrent review (running in parallel with the Phase 6 execution waves above). Independent review of the rebased branch's design and implementation BEFORE the coordinator merges.

SCOPE
- vigor-core/agent_config.py: schema correctness, validator completeness, security guard (allowed_prefixes), edge cases in the cross-field validators.
- vigor-agent/factory.py + registry.py: import-time failure modes, supply-chain guard depth, error message quality.
- vigor-agent/router.py: ambiguity handling, override precedence, missing-adapter behavior.
- vigor-mcp/backend.py: session lifecycle, timeout semantics under cancellation, allowlist enforcement at both list-time and call-time, error mapping from MCP isError to ToolResult.
- vigor-runtime/orchestrator.py: confirm 'tools' threading is the ONLY change; no loop semantics altered.
- ADR-0014 + ADR-0015: rejected alternatives credible? citations real? consequences accurate?
- Test coverage: which paths are NOT covered? (Look especially for HTTP transport, SSE transport, allowlist bypass attempts.)

DELIVERABLE
- Mail to coordinator with type=status containing prioritized findings (P0=blocker / P1=should-fix-before-merge / P2=follow-up).

ACCEPTANCE
- Every package on the branch reviewed (coverage explicit)
- ≥1 finding per package OR explicit 'no issues' note
- Findings either resolved by builders or filed as new Seeds tasks before coordinator merges
closeReason: Review complete. 3 reviewers reported: R1 (vigor-core, PASS-with-6-P1s), R2 (vigor-agent, FAIL-3-P0s/8-P1s), R3 (vigor-mcp+runtime, PASS-WITH-FINDINGS-2-P0s/5-P1s). Findings filed as Seeds bugs: VIGOR-81eb, VIGOR-8bdf, VIGOR-abf0, VIGOR-db22 (P0s), VIGOR-c931 (P1 bundle). Fix lead lead-framework-fixes spawned to resolve before merge into main.

description: Author docs/research/2026-mcp-reviewer-survey.md per VIGOR-5ec1. Subtask of VIGOR-5ec1.

title: Review R1: vigor-core agent_config + plugin + ADR-0014
description: Independent adversarial review of vigor-core changes on origin/claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV.
closeReason: PASS: vigor-core agent_config + plugin + ADR-0014 review complete. Tests 50/50 pass, ruff clean, no P0. 6 P1 findings (forward-compat docstring contradicts extra=forbid; allowed_prefixes uses raw startswith with no dot boundary in both core+harness; single-strategy + multi-adapter not schema-validated; transport-mismatched fields silently accepted on MCPServerSpec; missing enabled=False and allowlist-bypass test coverage), 5 P2 polish items. Full report in mail msg-es71dr5yarhx.

title: Review R2: vigor-agent (factory/registry/router) + ADR-0015
description: Independent adversarial review of vigor-agent package on origin/claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV.
closeReason: FAIL: 3 P0 (allowlist substring match in factory.py:29 — same bug in vigor-harness; eager load_factory in agent.py:49 doesn't deliver isinstance promise; plugin_discovery.py self-authorizes its own allowed_prefixes — supply-chain bypass for Open Plugin Spec plugins). 8 P1, 6 P2. 24/24 tests pass; ruff clean. Full report sent to lead-branch-review.

title: Review R3: vigor-mcp backend + vigor-runtime tools threading
description: Independent adversarial review of vigor-mcp + vigor-runtime tools-threading on origin/claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV.
closeReason: PASS-with-findings. Read-only adversarial review of vigor-mcp + vigor-runtime tools threading on origin/claude/agent-adapter-framework-1KOmV. All in-scope files reviewed; pytest 17/17 pass; ruff clean. Findings sent to lead-branch-review (R3 findings: vigor-mcp + runtime). 2 P0 (lifecycle leak via missing tools.aclose() in orchestrator finally; mutable allowlist), 5 P1 (timeout doesn't cancel MCP RPC; connect-failure stickiness; HTTP session_callback dropped; env/header injection surface; idiom inconsistency), 5 P2. Orchestrator threading confirmed surgical (8 lines, no semantics change).

title: Build: research Open Plugin Spec v1 host compatibility
description: Empirical research into which 2026 plugin hosts actually load OPS v1 plugins, where they expect MCP declarations, and what vendor manifest paths VIGOR should ship. Single-file deliverable: docs/research/open-plugin-spec-host-compatibility.md.
closeReason: Merged 2acd27e to main (no conflicts). Host compatibility audit landed at docs/research/open-plugin-spec-host-compatibility.md.

title: P0: allowed_prefixes uses raw startswith() — substring match, not security boundary
description:
DUPLICATED across vigor-agent/factory.py:29 and vigor-harness/evaluator.py:29. Both use module_name.startswith(prefix) with no dotted-component check. Confirmed empirically by reviewers R1+R2: allowed_prefixes=['vigor_runtime'] matches module 'vigor_runtime_evil.foo'.

ADR-0014 line 30 calls this a supply-chain guard but the implementation is a namespace assertion. Either:
1. Tighten matcher in BOTH loaders to dotted-component prefix matching (mirror stdlib pkgutil), OR
2. Soften ADR-0014 + agent_config.py:23-32 docstring language to 'namespace assertion'.

Recommend option 1 — security promise is correct, implementation must match.

Files to modify:
- packages/vigor-agent/src/vigor_agent/factory.py
- packages/vigor-harness/src/vigor_harness/evaluator.py
- Add negative tests to both packages' test files (typosquat module name)

Reviewers who flagged: R1 (msg-es71dr5yarhx), R2 (msg-dcms43wtyszh). MUST fix before VIGOR-d324 merge.
closeReason: Fixed in two parts: harness via VIGOR-83b7 (3cf1f6b), agent via VIGOR-b198 (5b2f891). Both copies of factory.py now use dotted-component prefix matching. Negative tests added for typosquat/exact/dotted-subpkg cases.

title: P0: plugin_discovery self-authorizes via attacker-supplied .plugin/vigor.json
description:
Third-party Open Plugin Spec plugins ship their own .plugin/vigor.json containing a FactoryRef with attacker-chosen allowed_prefixes. plugin_discovery.py:62-65 reads this file and trusts it. No host-side veto means the supply-chain guard is defeated for the entire ADR-0015 plugin pathway.

FIX
- AgentConfig must declare a host-side allowlist of acceptable prefixes for plugins (e.g. 'plugin_allowed_prefixes' field on AgentConfig).
- plugin_discovery.adapter_spec_from_plugin must intersect plugin-supplied allowed_prefixes with host-supplied allowlist and reject if empty intersection.
- Add test: plugin with allowed_prefixes=['evil'] is rejected when host allows only 'vigor_*'.

Reviewer who flagged: R2 (msg-dcms43wtyszh, P0 #3). MUST fix before VIGOR-d324 merge.
closeReason: Fixed via VIGOR-b198 (5b2f891). Added allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes field to AgentConfig + assert_factory_ref_allowed host gate. plugin_discovery.adapter_spec_from_plugin now requires host_allowed_prefixes parameter and rejects with PluginDiscoveryError on no-intersection. Tests cover positive (matching) and negative (attacker-supplied evil prefix) cases.

title: P0: MCP session leak — Orchestrator.finally never calls tools.aclose()
description:
vigor-runtime/orchestrator.py:212-214 finally block awaits backend.aclose() but NEVER calls self._tools.aclose(). MCPToolBackend.aclose() exists at vigor-mcp/backend.py:139-141 and tears down all per-server AsyncExitStacks (subprocess pipes for stdio, HTTP clients, SSE streams), but is never invoked.

Consequence: every Orchestrator.run() with tools=MCPToolBackend(...) leaks one MCP subprocess (stdio) or one open HTTP/SSE connection per configured server. In long-running agents this is a file-descriptor / zombie-process leak.

ToolBackend ABC at vigor-core/interfaces.py:130-138 has no aclose() method either — adapters that embed sessions today have no contract to close them.

FIX (preferred): Add async aclose() to ToolBackend ABC; orchestrator's finally awaits self._tools.aclose() if not None.
ALT: Document caller-owned tool lifecycle explicitly. AgentOrchestrator.aclose() already closes the tool_backend, so this is at least correct at the agent layer — but Orchestrator.run() called directly with tools=... still leaks.

Reviewer who flagged: R3 (msg-pruhqf8mq38y, P0-1). MUST fix before VIGOR-d324 merge.
closeReason: Fixed via VIGOR-b198 (5b2f891). ToolBackend.aclose() default no-op added to ABC; Orchestrator finally block now closes tools with contextlib.suppress. Test asserts aclose is awaited via mock ToolBackend.

title: P0: tool_allowlist mutable list is runtime-flippable
description:
vigor-mcp/backend.py call-time check uses handle.spec.tool_allowlist (a list). An attacker who can mutate the spec object at runtime (AgentConfig reload, hot-swap) flips the gate.

FIX: freeze tool_allowlist to frozenset at handle construction:
  self._allowlist = frozenset(spec.tool_allowlist) if spec.tool_allowlist else None

Apply at _ServerHandle.__init__ and use self._allowlist in list_tools and call_tool.

Reviewer who flagged: R3 (msg-pruhqf8mq38y, P0-2). MUST fix before VIGOR-d324 merge.
closeReason: Fixed via VIGOR-b198 (5b2f891). _ServerHandle materializes spec.tool_allowlist as frozenset at init; runtime spec mutation no longer flips the gate. Tests cover the immutability contract.

title: P1: agent_adapter-framework branch P1 findings — bundled fix
description:
Bundle of P1 findings from R1/R2/R3 to fix before merge. Each is a clear, scoped change.

R1 (vigor-core):
- P1-1: OpenPluginManifest forward-compat docstring contradicts extra='forbid' (plugin.py:32-36). Either override model_config to allow extras OR rewrite docstring.
- P1-3: RoutingPolicy='single' with multi-adapter accepted at schema layer; should fail in agent_config.py _check_unique_ids_and_routing.
- P1-4: MCPServerSpec accepts transport-irrelevant fields silently (e.g. command set with http transport).
- P1-5: enabled=False adapter not exercised in tests.
- P1-6: No allowlist negative tests.

R2 (vigor-agent):
- 8 P1s including non-transactional registry, narrow ImportError catches, no aclose idempotency, undocumented routing precedence, missing config_loader env-var expansion, missing negative tests.

R3 (vigor-mcp):
- P1-1: asyncio.wait_for doesn't propagate cancellation to MCP server; in-flight RPC stays live; next call_tool may dequeue prior response.
- P1-2: Connect failure leaves AsyncExitStack half-entered.
- P1-3: streamablehttp_client 3-tuple destructuring drops session_callback.
- P1-4: spec.env / spec.headers passed unchecked (LD_PRELOAD / Authorization injection).
- P1-5: Inconsistent dict(headers) or None idiom.

Full reports: msg-es71dr5yarhx (R1), msg-dcms43wtyszh (R2), msg-pruhqf8mq38y (R3). MUST fix before VIGOR-d324 merge or be explicitly deferred with ADR.
closeReason: P1 bundle fixed via VIGOR-b198 (5b2f891). Implemented: R1-P1-1 OpenPluginManifest extra=allow for forward-compat, R1-P1-3 single-strategy validator at schema layer, R1-P1-4 MCPServerSpec rejects transport-irrelevant fields (headers on stdio, env on http/sse), R3-P1-1 wait_for timeout tears down handle (alternative to JSON-RPC cancelled which SDK doesn't expose cleanly), R3-P1-2 AsyncExitStack rolls back on opener failure, R3-P1-3 explicit defensive shape check on streamablehttp_client tuple, R3-P1-4 env/headers transport guards added. 51 new tests, 142 total passing.

title: Tighten allowed_prefixes matcher in vigor_harness.evaluator to dotted-component prefix match
description: Builder subtask of VIGOR-81eb. Fix the substring-vs-namespace bug in packages/vigor-harness/src/vigor_harness/evaluator.py:29. Add negative test for typosquat module name.
closeReason: Merged 22f07ad to main as 3cf1f6b. Harness factory.py allowed_prefixes tightened to dotted-component matcher with negative tests.

title: P0+P1 agent-branch fixes (rooted in rebase worktree, not main)
description:
Sibling of VIGOR-81eb. Apply the agent-side portion of the P0 + P1 review findings to the rebased framework branch (commit 04d57d0 on overstory/builder-rebase-agent-framework/VIGOR-67ca). The builder for this task MUST work in or branch from the rebase worktree, NOT from main, because the agent code only exists on that branch.

SCOPE
- VIGOR-81eb agent-side: vigor-agent/factory.py:29 — dotted-component prefix match. Add negative tests.
- VIGOR-8bdf: vigor-agent/plugin_discovery.py + vigor-core/agent_config.py — host-side allowlist for plugin-supplied prefixes. Add tests.
- VIGOR-abf0: vigor-runtime/orchestrator.py finally block + vigor-core/interfaces.py ToolBackend.aclose. Add tests.
- VIGOR-db22: vigor-mcp/backend.py — frozenset allowlist. Update tests.
- VIGOR-c931 R3 portion: vigor-mcp/backend.py — wait_for cancellation, AsyncExitStack rollback, streamablehttp tuple, env/headers validation.
- VIGOR-c931 R1 portion: vigor-core agent_config.py — single-strategy validator, MCPServerSpec transport-irrelevant fields, OpenPluginManifest forward-compat.
- ADR-0014 line 30: change 'supply-chain guard' to 'namespace assertion + dotted-component prefix match' for technical accuracy.

BASE: branch from overstory/builder-rebase-agent-framework/VIGOR-67ca (commit 04d57d0).
ACCEPTANCE: ruff, format, mypy, pytest all green; merge_ready signal to coordinator with branch name.
DO NOT touch the harness — that's covered by VIGOR-83b7 (separate builder).
closeReason: Implemented P0+P1 agent-branch fixes: dotted-component factory prefix matching, host-side plugin allowlist, ToolBackend.aclose, frozenset MCP allowlist, wait_for timeout handle rollback, AsyncExitStack ensure_open rollback, transport-irrelevant MCPServerSpec field validation, single-strategy validator, OpenPluginManifest forward-compat, ADR-0014 wording fix. 142 tests pass; ruff/format/mypy strict all green. See commit ea7e0f3.

title: Architect: ADR-0018 vendor manifest dual-publishing for OPS host compatibility
description:
The empirical host-compat research (docs/research/open-plugin-spec-host-compatibility.md) shows ZERO hosts honor the vendor-neutral .plugin/plugin.json path that ADR-0015 picked as the source-of-truth manifest. Only Claude Code loads .claude-plugin/plugin.json. 7 hosts use proprietary schemes.

This ADR amends ADR-0015's defer-vendor-paths decision: dual-publish vendor manifests from the same generator at minimum for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Hermes.

DEPENDENCIES
- Should land AFTER the framework branch merges (so we have the existing manifests to extend)

CONTENT
- Decision: dual-publish via scripts/regen_skills.py (or successor) that emits .plugin/plugin.json (source) + .claude-plugin/plugin.json + gemini-extension.json + Hermes plugin.yaml from one shared template.
- ≥2 considered options: single-manifest (current — empirically loads in 0 hosts), dual-publish (recommended), single-vendor pivot (rejected), wait-for-OPS-PR-3 (rejected).
- Pure-MCP plugin subsection deferred to ADR-0017 (already landed).
- Cite docs/research/open-plugin-spec-host-compatibility.md and OPS PR #3.

ACCEPTANCE
- docs/adr/0018-vendor-manifest-dual-publishing.md, MADR 3.0, status=proposed
- Explicitly notes it amends ADR-0015's 'Defer .claude-plugin/plugin.json' decision
- Concrete generator design (file paths, template format)
closeReason: Authored ADR-0018 (vendor manifest dual-publishing) at docs/adr/0018-vendor-manifest-dual-publishing.md, MADR 3.0, status=proposed. Frontmatter 'refines: ADR-0015' amends only the deferral-of-vendor-paths row of ADR-0015's Alternatives table. 4 considered options with full pros/cons. Concrete generator design specified (file paths, per-target field projection, drift guard, pinned schema-revision matrix). Index updated. Committed 0c5e0ea.

title: Wire AgentOrchestrator.allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes through to plugin_discovery (VIGOR-8bdf consumer integration)
description:
VIGOR-8bdf landed the host-gate primitive (assert_factory_ref_allowed) and the AgentConfig field (allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes), but AgentOrchestrator does not yet pass the prefixes through to plugin_discovery.adapter_spec_from_plugin at construction time.

WORK
- packages/vigor-agent/src/vigor_agent/agent.py: AgentOrchestrator.__init__ should expose discover_plugin or load_plugin_directory wiring that reads config.allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes and passes to adapter_spec_from_plugin's host_allowed_prefixes parameter.
- Add integration test: AgentConfig with allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes=['vigor_*'] + a plugin directory whose .plugin/vigor.json has allowed_prefixes=['evil'] is rejected at construction time.

This closes the loop: data plane and primitive are in place from VIGOR-8bdf; this task wires the consumer side.

title: Pre-existing pytest failures: optional-SDK backends raise unexpectedly when SDKs are installed
description:
Two tests fail when uv sync --all-packages --all-extras installs the optional Strands and Claude Agent SDK packages:

  packages/vigor-backend-claude-agent-sdk/tests/test_backend.py::test_generate_raises_without_sdk
  packages/vigor-backend-strands/tests/test_backend.py::test_generate_raises_without_strands

Failure mechanism: tests assert ImportError when the optional SDK is absent, but uv sync --all-extras installs the SDKs, so the tests hit live AWS Bedrock instead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Use a pytest fixture to mock the SDK module to be absent (sys.modules['strands']=None, similar for claude_agent_sdk) for these specific test functions.
- OR mark the tests as requires_no_sdk and skip when the SDK is importable.

Pre-existing on main; surfaced by builder-rebase-agent-framework (VIGOR-67ca quality gate run). Out of scope of the framework merge but should be filed for cleanup.
